concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII mandates: 'a random subset of tagged blocks MUST be manually verified and stored as a ground-truth dataset... The classifier's precision and recall on this subset MUST be reported'. This requirement has no corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) or Success Criterion (SC) in the spec. FR-002 covers the classifier execution, but no FR/SC addresses the manual verification workflow, ground-truth storage, or the reporting of precision/recall metrics required by the project constitution.

User Story 1 requires establishing a 'valid baseline population' and 'tagging specific code blocks'. While FR-001/002/003 cover cloning and tagging, there is no explicit FR or SC addressing the 'matching' or 'stratification' logic mentioned in the Idea Md ('Match LLM-generated blocks with human-written blocks from the same repository... to control for project-specific variables'). Without an FR/SC for the matching algorithm, the 'controlling for code complexity and project size' goal in the research question lacks a concrete implementation requirement.
